---
title: "Replicating UN Voting Ideal Points in Julia"
author: "Valeria Farinola"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
output-file: index.html
---

# Overview

This project replicates the Bayesian ideal point estimation procedure from Bailey, Strezhnev, and Voeten (2017), *Estimating Dynamic State Preferences from United Nations Voting Data*.

The goal is to reproduce the estimated United Nations voting ideal points using Julia, compute dyadic ideological distances, and replicate the figure showing the ideal points of the five permanent members of the UN Security Council.

# Original paper and replication package

The original replication package is available at:

<https://github.com/evoeten/United-Nations-General-Assembly-Votes-and-Ideal-Points>

## Julia replication repository

The Julia replication package developed for this project is available at:

<https://github.com/valeriafarinola/UNIdealPointsJulia>

# Replication goals

The project has three main goals:

1. Replicate the MCMC ideal point estimator implemented in `UN_Ideal_Rcpp.R`.
2. Reproduce country-session ideal point estimates.
3. Compute dyadic ideological distances and replicate the P5 (five permanent members of the UN Security Council) ideal points figure.

# Model summary

The estimator is a Bayesian ideal point model for United Nations voting data.

Each country-session receives a latent ideal point, denoted as theta. Votes are modeled through a latent continuous variable and threshold parameters. The sampler updates theta, gamma thresholds, latent Z variables, and beta discrimination parameters.

# Julia implementation

The Julia implementation includes:

- theta update;
- dynamic lagged prior;
- gamma threshold Metropolis-Hastings update;
- latent Z update;
- beta update;
- MCMC loop with burn-in and thinning;
- posterior storage;
- ideal point export.

# Repository structure

The repository is organized into four main components:

- `src/`
  - core Julia functions implementing the Bayesian ideal point estimator;

- `scripts/`
  - executable replication scripts for estimation, plotting, diagnostics, and post-processing;

- `data/`
  - processed intermediate inputs generated by the original R/Rcpp replication package;

- `output/`
  - generated Julia estimation outputs and dyadic ideological distances.

## Main scripts

- `run_mcmc_full_all.jl`
  - runs the full Bayesian MCMC estimation for the All-votes dataset;

- `plot_p5.jl`
  - reproduces the P5 ideal point figure for the All-votes dataset;

- `plot_p5_important.jl`
  - reproduces the P5 ideal point figure for the Important-votes dataset;

- `compute_dyadic_distances.jl`
  - computes pairwise ideological distances between countries within each UNGA session;

- `compare_theta.jl`
  - compares Julia-estimated ideal points against the original R replication output.

# Main replication output

The full All-votes MCMC run used:

- K = 20000
- Burn = 4000
- Thin = 50

The final Julia ideal point estimates are stored in:


```text
output/ThetaEst_full_all.csv
```
# Replicated P5 figures

## All votes dataset

### Original replication output

![](figures/Figure1All_Apr2020.png){width=70%}

### Julia replication

![](figures/p5_ideal_points_julia.png){width=70%}

The Julia replication reproduces the main substantive dynamics of the original replication output, including the relative positioning of the United States, USSR/Russia, China, the United Kingdom, and France across UNGA sessions.

## Important votes dataset

### Original replication output

![](figures/Figure1Important_Apr2020.png){width=70%}

### Julia replication

![](figures/p5_ideal_points_julia_important.png){width=70%}

The Julia replication based on the Important-votes subset reproduces the broad structure of the original replication output while reflecting differences due to MCMC sampling variation and implementation details.

# Dyadic ideological distances

Dyadic distances are computed as a post-estimation transformation:

```text
distance(i,j) = |theta_i - theta_j|
```

The dyadic distance output is stored in:

```text
output/dyadic_distances_full_all.csv
```

# Package tests

The package includes a small test suite covering:

- R-style repetition helper;
- dyadic distance formula.

The tests can be run with:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.test()
```
In addition to unit tests, the repository also includes a lightweight MCMC smoke test (`scripts/run_mcmc_smoke_test.jl`) for quickly verifying end-to-end sampler execution and repository reproducibility.


# Conclusion

The Julia implementation successfully reproduces the main computational steps of the original R/Rcpp estimator and generates ideal point estimates for the All-votes dataset. The replicated P5 figure reproduces the main substantive dynamics of the original paper.
